channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: A Weekend Exploring the World of Hackers, Scammers, and Online Deception
item.description: This past weekend I decided to explore something many of us hear about but rarely understand deeply — how hackers and online scammers actually gain access to people’s devices, accounts, and...

item.title: Pattern Matching vs Manual Casting in C#
item.description: When working with polymorphism in C#, you often deal with a base type that can represent many different derived types. For example, in a payment system you might have a base class Payment, and...

item.title: The Circuit Breaker Pattern: Stop Your AI Agent From Amplifying Failures
item.description: Every experienced engineer knows the circuit breaker pattern for microservices. But most AI agent builders ignore it entirely. The Problem: Agents Amplify Failures A typical agent loop: fetch...

item.title: I'm Not a Developer — So I Built a Movie App in 2 Months with AI
item.description: I'll be honest with you: I don't know how to code. No JavaScript, no Python, no SQL. I can't read a stack trace and I wouldn't know what a pull request looks like. And yet, two months ago I...

item.title: What 3 Years of Stack Overflow Data Tells Us About AI and Machine Learning in Development
item.description: Every year Stack Overflow surveys tens of thousands of developers worldwide. Three years of that data now tells a surprisingly nuanced story about AI and machine learning — one that's very...

item.title: Built 5 apps in 20 Days
item.description: Hey there Welcome to the new email. The last 30 days have been crazy because I've been working on multiple ideas at the same time using one boilerplate. I ended up making 5 apps in the last 20...

item.title: Infinite Scrolling in React Native or Expo with Zustic Query
item.description: Infinite scrolling is one of the most common UX patterns in mobile apps. Instead of loading hundreds of records at once, we load data page by page as the user scrolls. In this article, we'll build...

item.title: AI Might Create a New Job: The Developer Inside Every Business
item.description: For the past two years we've been asking the wrong question about AI and software development. The question everyone keeps asking is: Will AI replace developers? But after spending time building...

item.title: Stop Searching for Documentation: Why We Stripped the Settings Screens from Rizm
item.description: Whenever a team adopts a new tool, an invisible burden is created: the dependency on a "tool specialist." We've all seen it—people spending hours learning proprietary specs or even getting "admin...

item.title: Workshop: Auditing Your Kubernetes Costs in 30 Minutes
item.description: What We'll Build By the end of this workshop, you'll have a concrete cost audit of your Kubernetes cluster — actual utilization numbers, a cost-per-value ratio, and a clear action plan. You'll...

item.title: Web Server Exploits and Mimikatz Used in Attacks Targeting Asian Critical Infrastructure
item.description: High-value organizations located in South, Southeast, and East Asia have been targeted by a Chinese threat actor as part of a years-long campaign. The activity, which has targeted aviation,...

item.title: Ring’s Jamie Siminoff has been trying to calm privacy fears since the Super Bowl, but his answers may not help
item.description: The facial recognition question is where things get more tangled.

item.title: How AI Assistants are Moving the Security Goalposts
item.description: AI-based assistants or "agents" -- autonomous programs that have access to the user's computer, files, online services and can automate virtually any task -- are growing in popularity with...

item.title: Will the Pentagon’s Anthropic controversy scare startups away from defense work?
item.description: On the latest episode of TechCrunch’s Equity podcast, we discussed what the controversy means for other startups seeking to work with the federal government.

item.title: Apple is going high-end with new ‘Ultra’ products next
item.description: Fresh off launching the low-cost MacBook Neo, Apple is reportedly preparing at least three new products that will fit into its highest-end "ultra" lineup. According to Bloomberg's Mark Gruman, the...

item.title: Owner of ICE detention facility sees big opportunity in AI man camps
item.description: AI data center developers are increasingly relying on a style of camp popularized as housing for men working in remote oil fields.

item.title: Listen to this: Mabe Fratti’s experimental cello pop
item.description: The opening notes of "Kravitz", which kicks off Mabe Fratti's 2024 record Sentir Que No Sabes, are lodged in my brain permanently. It's not a showy album, by any means. But there's something about...

item.title: What we’re listening to, watching, and reading right now.
item.description: There is so much art out there that it’s absolutely impossible to keep up. Whether it’s a slept-on post-punk album from the ‘80s, a new sci-fi novel, or a cult classic horror movie, we’re always...

item.title: The best Switch 2 controller just got better (and a little worse)
item.description: Crowning the EasySMX S10 the best Switch 2 controller last fall was easy. The $60 wireless gamepad, which is often well below $50, mimics or improves on many of the $90 Switch 2 Pro controller's...

item.title: The cute and cursed story of Furby
item.description: The hottest toy of 1998 was sort of adorable, and sort of annoying. It couldn't do much - couldn't do anything, really - but it could look at you, it could say some nonsense phrases, and it seemed...

item.title: The uncomfortable truth about hybrid vehicles
item.description: This is The Stepback, a weekly newsletter breaking down one essential story from the tech world. For more on the EV struggle, follow Andrew J. Hawkins. The Stepback arrives in our subscribers'...

item.title: A roadmap for AI, if anyone will listen
item.description: The Pro-Human Declaration was finalized before last week's Pentagon-Anthropic standoff, but the collision of the two events wasn’t lost on anyone involved.

item.title: Google just gave Sundar Pichai a $692M pay package
item.description: Most of it is tied to performance, including new stock incentives linked to Waymo and Wing, its drone delivery venture.

item.title: Grammarly’s ‘expert review’ is just missing the actual experts
item.description: A recently-added feature in Grammarly purports to improve users’ writing with help from the world's great writers and thinkers — and some tech journalists, too.

item.title: OpenAI hardware exec Caitlin Kalinowski quits in response to Pentagon deal
item.description: Hardware executive Caitlin Kalinowski announced today that in response to OpenAI's controversial agreement with the Department of Defense, she’s resigned from her role leading the company's...

item.title: Sony appears to be testing dynamic pricing on PlayStation games
item.description: A site called PSprices has been tracking prices on Sony's digital game store and noticed something unusual: some games were being offered at different prices to different users. What's more, those...

item.title: OpenAI delays ChatGPT’s ‘adult mode’ again
item.description: The feature, which will give verified adult users access to erotica and other adult content, had already been delayed from December.

item.title: Time’s running out to get a free gift card when you preorder a new MacBook
item.description: This has been a week to remember here at The Verge. MWC 2026 in Barcelona lead straight into Apple’s week of product announcements. There was truly something for everyone, whether you’ve been...

item.title: OpenAI Codex Security Scanned 1.2 Million Commits and Found 10,561 High-Severity Issues
item.description: OpenAI on Friday began rolling out Codex Security, an artificial intelligence (AI)-powered security agent that's designed to find, validate, and propose fixes for vulnerabilities. The feature is...

item.title: Vizio accounts are becoming Walmart accounts
item.description: Walmart bought Vizio in 2024, and now it's taking the next steps to formally fold the TV maker into itself by phasing out Vizio accounts. Now, when you purchase a new Vizio TV, you'll be asked to...
